definition: Candles with disproportionately long wicks at key levels — the wick zone represents where institutional orders absorbed aggressive selling/buying and rejected the price.
feeds_into: trade entry (rejection block wick zone = entry area on retest)
related_to: order_blocks (rejection blocks are a TYPE of block, but identified by wick behavior, not body)
swing_failure_pattern (SFP candles often ARE rejection blocks)
fair_value_gaps (a rejection block's wick can create a gap that acts like an FVG)
definition_detail: A candle where the wick is >60% of the total range. The wick zone (from wick tip to body edge) is the rejection block.
bullish_rejection: Long lower wick at support. The wick zone below the body = demand zone. Enter on retest of this zone.
bearish_rejection: Long upper wick at resistance. The wick zone above the body = supply zone. Enter on retest.
quality: Rejection blocks at PDH/PDL, OB zones, or FVG boundaries are highest quality. Random rejection blocks are less significant.
